name: Buddy Childers
abstract: Marion "Buddy" Childers (February 12, 1926 – May 24, 2007) was an American jazz trumpeter, composer and ensemble leader. Childers became famous in 1942 at the age of 16, when Stan Kenton hired him to be the lead trumpet in his band.

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Findley
about_quotes.evidence: So I’ll tell you on that session there were three trumpets. It was myself, it was Ollie Mitchell, and Buddy Childers. The three of us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Findley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 6, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: I had Buddy Childers and Ralph Pena Pinaud, Art Anton and Bob Harrington, and we were supposed to play in the lounge

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: The next year, '52, I think it was Stan Kenton, who also had a great band at this time. This was one of his great bands. Let's see, I think it was Bob Burgess, Buddy Childers, Don Bagby --

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, those who come to mind right off was Willie Green, Buddy Collette, Grover Mitchell, Conte Candoli, Buddy Childers, Irv Kotler playing drums, and... It was a big band.

about_quotes.speaker: Don Menza and Sam Noto
about_quotes.evidence: Buddy Childers was the first trumpet player, and we had a little rehearsal... So I went out and Conte decided not to come back... that’s how I stayed on the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Menza and Sam Noto interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Porrello
about_quotes.evidence: But I worked with him and actually I replaced Buddy Childers there. Buddy Childers was the trumpet player for Vic and I replaced him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Porrello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 6, 2023
